risk_if_not_implemented: Without Biometric Categorization, residual security, compliance, or resilience risk may accumulate, potentially leading to control failures or non-compliance.

compensating_control_1.name: Security, Compliance & Resilience Assessments
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to regularly review processes and documented procedures to ensure conformity with the organization's security, compliance and/or resilience policies, standards and other applicable requirements.
compensating_control_1.justification: Security, Compliance & Resilience Assessments (CPL-03) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of Biometric Categorization (AAT-19.8) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to facilitate the identification and implementation of relevant statutory, regulatory and contractual controls.
compensating_control_2.justification: Statutory, Regulatory & Contractual Compliance (CPL-01) provides overlapping security capability that compensates for the absence of Biometric Categorization (AAT-19.8) by addressing related risk objectives through an alternative control mechanism aligned with Process applicability. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
